values.value: Flags newly issued OauthToken rows whose AppName is absent from the user's warmed-up baseline App_Set__c. Users granting consent to apps they have never authorized before is the dominant signal of an OAuth phishing campaign, especially when paired with surge or broad-scope findings on the same application.

Inspect the connected app: publisher, scopes requested, install base, and creation date. Revoke the user's token if the app is unknown or scope is excessive. If multiple users in a short window authorized the same unknown app, treat as a campaign and block the connected app org-wide.
